name: Atami
description: Atami is a scenic destination in the Shizuoka region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.
reservation: Essential during summer and fireworks days.
parking: Limited near the beach.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Seaside & Art
itineraries.description: A mix of ocean views and world-class museums.

steps.activity: Arrive in Atami. Take the bus to the spectacular MOA Museum of Art.
Lunch near Atami Sun Beach, enjoying fresh local seafood.
Take the ropeway up to Atami Castle for panoramic bay views.
Explore the retro shopping streets near the station and eat onsen manju.
Take a quick dip in a public bath before taking the Shinkansen home.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Retro Atami Vibe
itineraries.description: Focus on the nostalgic Showa-era atmosphere of this classic resort town.

steps.activity: Visit Kiunkaku, a gorgeous former ryokan loved by Japanese literary giants.
Eat a classic Japanese curry or omurice at a retro Kissaten (coffee shop).
Visit Kinomiya Shrine to see the 2,000-year-old sacred camphor tree.
Stroll along the oceanfront and find a quiet day-use hot spring.
Dinner at a local izakaya in the old town.

notes: Check the schedule for Atami's year-round fireworks displays!

description: Boso Peninsula is a scenic destination in the Chiba region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Mountain & Buddha
itineraries.description: A hiking and cultural adventure on Mt. Nokogiri.

steps.activity: Arrive at Hamakanaya and take the Mt. Nokogiri Ropeway up the mountain.
Hike to the Jigoku Nozoki (Hell's Peek) viewpoint.
Descend into the quarry to see the massive Nihon-ji Daibutsu (Great Buddha).
Late lunch of fresh caught seafood near the ferry terminal.
Relax at the Baya-no-yu oceanfront hot spring.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Coastline Road Trip
itineraries.description: Best done by car. Exploring the scenic drives of Chiba.

steps.activity: Drive the Tokyo Bay Aqua-Line and stop at Umihotaru PA.
Visit Mother Farm to see the animals and flower fields.
Drive down to Katsuura or Kamogawa for an incredible seafood lunch.
Explore the Kamogawa Sea World or take a coastal drive to the lighthouse.
Watch the sunset over the Pacific Ocean.

notes: A fantastic trip to Boso Peninsula. Known for its road trip and ocean vibes, it offers a great escape with an overall score of 9.7.

Ride Indiana Jones Adventure and Raging Spirits.

Ride the Transit Steamer Line at sunset for beautiful views.

Visit Enoshima Shrine. Take the escalators up if you want to skip the stairs.
Eat fresh Shirasu-don for lunch.

name: Ginza
description: Ginza is a district of Chūō, Tokyo, located south of Yaesu and Kyōbashi, west of Tsukiji, east of Yūrakuchō and Uchisaiwaichō, and north of Shinbashi. It is a popular upscale shopping area of Tokyo, with numerous internationally renowned department stores, boutiques, restaurants and coffeehouses located in the vicinity.
parking: Expensive.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Cultured Urban Stroll
itineraries.description: Discover high-quality Japanese craftsmanship, from precision watches to perfect paper.

steps.activity: Explore the Seiko Museum Ginza to learn about the history of Japanese timepieces.
Lunch at a traditional tempura or sushi restaurant.
Spend an hour wandering the 12 floors of Itoya, the ultimate stationery store.
Relax at a classic kissaten (retro cafe) with hand-drip coffee.
Walk the Chuo-dori pedestrian paradise (weekends only).

notes: Main street is closed to traffic on weekends (Pedestrian Paradise).

name: Hakone
description: Hakone is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.
reservation: Required well in advance for ryokans.
parking: Limited and expensive.

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Classic Hakone Loop
itineraries.description: Experience all modes of transport and the best views of Mt. Fuji.

steps.activity: Arrive at Hakone-Yumoto and take the scenic Tozan Mountain Railway to Gora.
Transfer to the Hakone Ropeway at Sounzan. Enjoy views over the volcanic Owakudani valley.
Eat black eggs (Kuro-tamago) boiled in the hot springs at Owakudani.
Take the Pirate Ship cruise across Lake Ashi to Motohakone.
Walk the old Cedar Avenue and visit Hakone Shrine.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Art & Relaxation
itineraries.description: A laid-back day focusing on world-class museums and high-end hot springs.

steps.activity: Explore the Hakone Open-Air Museum, featuring Picasso and massive outdoor sculptures.
Lunch at a traditional soba restaurant in Gora.
Visit the Pola Museum of Art, nestled deep in the forest.
Enjoy a premium day-use Onsen (hot spring) with mountain views.
Dinner in Hakone-Yumoto before heading back to Yokohama.

notes: Purchase the Hakone Free Pass for unlimited rides on local transport.

name: Harry Potter Studio
description: Harry Potter Studio is a scenic destination in the Tokyo region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.
parking: Available but train recommended

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Ultimate Fan Experience
itineraries.description: A comprehensive walk-through prioritizing all major sets and photo ops.

steps.activity: Arrive early and enter the Great Hall.
Explore the Hogwarts Express and Platform 9 ¾.
Drink Butterbeer in the Backlot Cafe.
Walk through Diagon Alley and the Ministry of Magic sets.
Spend an hour in the massive studio gift shop.

itineraries.name: Plan B: The Filmmaker's Focus
itineraries.description: Take your time studying the props, costumes, and behind-the-scenes magic.

steps.activity: Focus heavily on the Art Department and Costume exhibits.
Participate in the interactive green-screen broomstick flying.
Lunch at the Food Hall.
Study the creature effects and animatronics in the Creature Shop.
Examine the incredibly detailed Hogwarts Castle miniature.

notes: Book ahead

itineraries.description: Historical architecture and traditional river sights.

Climb Inuyama Castle, a National Treasure, for views of the Kiso River.
Enjoy street food snacks or traditional soba for lunch.
Stroll through Urakuen Garden and enjoy matcha at the Joan tea house.
Take a boat ride on the Kiso River.

notes: A perfect extension to a Nagoya trip, offering one of Japan's oldest surviving wooden castles.

parking: Yes

itineraries.name: Plan A: Thrills & Coasters
itineraries.description: Tackle the most intense, adrenaline-pumping rides the indoor park has to offer.

steps.activity: Arrive at Decks Tokyo Beach and head into Joypolis.
Ride the Gekion Live Coaster, a rhythm game combined with a rollercoaster.
Compete against friends on the Halfpipe Tokyo spinning ride.
Quick lunch at the Frame Cafe inside the park overlooking the bay.
Ride the Transformers 360-degree spinning simulator.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Horror & Virtual Reality
itineraries.description: Focus on the immersive walk-through attractions and cutting-edge VR.

steps.activity: Start with the Zero Latency VR free-roaming experience (book in advance!).
Grab lunch at a restaurant in Odaiba outside the park.
Experience the Room of the Living Doll 3D sound horror attraction.
Play the interactive Phoenix Wright: Ace Attorney investigation game.
Watch the main stage projection mapping show before leaving.

name: Kamakura
description: Kamakura , officially Kamakura City , is a city of Kanagawa Prefecture in Japan. It is located in the Kantō region on the island of Honshu. The city has an estimated population of 172,929 and a population density of 4,359 people per km2 over the total area of 39.67 km2 (15.32 sq mi). Kamakura was designated as a city on 3 November 1939.
parking: Extremely congested on weekends.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Samurai Heritage Tour
itineraries.description: The classic Kamakura route focusing on the most famous shrines and the Great Buddha.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kamakura Station and walk up the iconic Wakamiya Oji approach.
Visit Tsurugaoka Hachimangu, the most important Shinto shrine in Kamakura.
Street food and souvenir shopping along Komachi-dori street.
Take the Enoden train to Hase Station. Visit the giant bronze Great Buddha.
Explore Hase-dera temple and its beautiful hillside gardens and ocean views.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Bamboo Groves & Ocean Breezes
itineraries.description: Avoid the main crowds by exploring eastern Kamakura and the nearby island of Enoshima.

steps.activity: Take a bus to Hokoku-ji Temple. Enjoy matcha tea in the serene bamboo grove.
Lunch at a quiet local cafe in the Jomyoji area.
Ride the Enoden train all the way to Enoshima.
Walk across the bridge to Enoshima Island and explore the shrine and caves.
Watch the sunset over Mt. Fuji from the Enoshima Sea Candle.

notes: Wear comfortable walking shoes. Enoden line can be very crowded.

parking: Available but traffic can be slow in town.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kawagoe Station and take the retro loop bus.
Stroll the Kurazukuri Zone (Warehouse District) and photograph the Toki no Kane (Time Bell Tower).

name: Omiya Railway Museum
description: Ōmiya is one of ten wards of the city of Saitama, in Saitama Prefecture, Japan, and is located in the northeastern part of the city. As of 1 March 2021, the ward had an estimated population of 119,298 and a population density of 9,300 persons per km2. Its total area was 12.80 square kilometres (4.94 sq mi). Although Urawa-ku is the governmental center of Saitama City, Ōmiya-ku is the most active commercial and business centre in both Saitama City and Saitama Prefecture thanks to its transport infrastructure, especially railways connected at Ōmiya Station.
parking: Yes

itineraries.name: Plan A: The Train Otaku

steps.activity: Arrive at the museum and head straight to the massive Rolling Stock Station.
Book and experience the Steam Locomotive or Shinkansen Simulator.

Watch the massive Railway Diorama show.
Explore the History Zone and learn about the evolution of the Shinkansen.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Family Fun Day
itineraries.description: A relaxed pace focused on interactive exhibits perfect for kids.

steps.activity: Arrive and immediately head to the Mini Driving Train area.
Lunch at the Kids Cafe, which has train-themed meals.
Let the kids play in the Teppaku Playroom and outdoor park.
Take family photos with the historic steam engines.
Buy exclusive train toys at the museum shop before leaving.

notes: Excellent hot-day option

description: Sado Island is an island located in the eastern part of the Sea of Japan, under the jurisdiction of Sado City, Niigata Prefecture, Japan, with a coastline of 262.7 kilometres (163.2 mi). Sado Island covers an area of 854.76 km2 (330.02 sq mi), and is the sixth largest island in Japan, excluding the disputed Southern Kurils. name: Utsunomiya & Oya
description: Utsunomiya & Oya is a scenic destination in the Tochigi region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.
parking: Yes

itineraries.name: Plan A: Stone & Gyoza
itineraries.description: The perfect mix of exploring massive underground caves and eating famous local food.

steps.activity: Take a bus from Utsunomiya Station to the Oya History Museum.
Explore the massive, atmospheric underground stone quarry.
Visit the nearby Oya-ji Temple and the giant Heiwa Kannon statue.
Head back to central Utsunomiya.
Go 'Gyoza Hopping' – try 3 different famous gyoza shops for a late lunch/dinner.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Scenic Cycling
itineraries.description: Rent a bike and explore the rural beauty surrounding the stone town.

steps.activity: Rent a bicycle near Utsunomiya Station.
Cycle through the scenic countryside towards the Oya district.
Lunch at a stylish cafe built into an old stone warehouse.
Explore the ancient cliff carvings at Oya-ji Temple.
Cycle back, drop off the bike, and reward yourself with gyoza.

notes: Oya museum is very cool in summer

name: Yokohama
description: Yokohama is a scenic destination in the Kanagawa region of Japan, offering rich history and cultural attractions.
reservation: Dinners with night views require booking.
parking: Plenty of paid parking in Minatomirai.

itineraries.name: Plan A: Minatomirai Classics
itineraries.description: The ultimate waterfront date or family trip hitting all major landmarks.

steps.activity: Design your own custom cup noodles at the Cup Noodle Museum.
Lunch and shopping at the historic Red Brick Warehouse.
Take a ride on the Cosmo Clock 21 Ferris wheel.
Take the Yokohama Air Cabin ropeway across the bay.
Dinner in Yokohama Chinatown, the largest in Japan.

itineraries.name: Plan B: Gardens & History
itineraries.description: A quieter side of Yokohama, focusing on classic Japanese gardens and port history.

steps.activity: Explore Sankeien Garden, featuring historic buildings relocated from across Japan.
Lunch in the elegant Motomachi Shopping Street.
Stroll up to the Yamate Bluffs to see the historic Western-style diplomatic houses.
Walk through Yamashita Park and tour the NYK Hikawa Maru ocean liner.
Cocktails at a high-rise bar in the Landmark Tower.

notes: Extremely easy access from central Tokyo.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Yokohama via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Yomiuriland
description: Yomiuriland is an amusement park in Inagi, Tokyo and Tama-ku, Kawasaki, Kanagawa Prefecture, Japan that first opened on 19 March 1964. It is situated on hillsides, and features rides such as roller coasters and water flumes. It is home to Yomiuri Giants Stadium, one of the training fields for the Yomiuri Giants baseball team, and was the primary training ground before Tokyo Dome was completed. It is operated and run by Yomiuriland Co., Ltd., a wholly owned subsidiary of The Yomiuri Shimbun Holdings.
parking: Available, but crowded in winter.

itineraries.description: The most efficient way to see the highlights of Yomiuriland in one day.

Visit the primary theme park attraction.

Relax at a local café before heading back.

notes: Winter Jewellumination is incredibly famous and romantic.

name: Tokyo Station
description: Japans premier railway terminal, featuring the restored 1914 Marunouchi red-brick facade, subterranean shopping passages, world-class ramen eateries, and immediate proximity to the Imperial Palace grounds.
reservation: Not required for public station concourses; reservations recommended for high-end Marunouchi dining.
parking: Paid underground parking garages available throughout the Marunouchi and Yaesu districts.

itineraries.name: Marunouchi & Station Heritage Walk
itineraries.description: Explore red-brick architecture, underground gourmet streets, and Imperial Palace gardens.

steps.activity: Admire the restored 1914 Marunouchi station dome and grand atrium.
Walk through Tokyo Character Street and shop for regional souvenirs.
Enjoy lunch at Tokyo Ramen Street in First Avenue station underground.
Stroll down Marunouchi Naka-dori boulevard to the Imperial Palace East Gardens.
Relax at a rooftop station cafe with views of incoming Shinkansen trains.

notes: Direct access to Narita Express and all Shinkansen lines. Excellent rain-proof indoor routing.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Tokyo Station via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Takanawa Gateway
highlights: TOUCH TO GO Automated Convenience Store
description: Designed by world-renowned architect Kengo Kuma, Takanawa Gateway is the newest station on Tokyos iconic Yamanote Line, featuring a dramatic paper-origami glass roof, AI-powered unmanned stores, and futuristic urban planning.
parking: Short-term parking garages available nearby at Shinagawa Station and Takanawa Gateway City development.

itineraries.name: Architectural & Innovation Discovery Walk
itineraries.description: Experience futuristic Tokyo transit architecture and automated shopping in Minato ward.

steps.activity: Arrive via JR Yamanote Line and marvel at the massive timber-framed origami roof canopy.
Experience TOUCH TO GO, Japans pioneer self-checkout AI convenience store.
Walk to nearby Sengakuji Temple, famous burial site of the 47 Ronin.
Enjoy artisan coffee at the elevated station concourse cafe overlooking train tracks.

notes: Station opened in 2020 as the 30th Yamanote Line station. Beautiful night illumination of the cedar wood roof structure.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Takanawa Gateway Station via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Tokyo Skytree
description: Rising 634 meters over Tokyo, Tokyo Skytree is the world's tallest broadcasting tower, offering breath-taking 360-degree views of Mt. Fuji and the Kanto Plain from two observation decks.
reservation: Advance online booking recommended to skip ticket queues.
parking: Paid underground parking structure at Tokyo Solamachi complex.

itineraries.name: Skytree Heights & Solamachi Experience
itineraries.description: Ascend Japan's highest tower deck and explore over 300 shops and cafes in Solamachi.

steps.activity: Arrive at Oshiage Station and ride the high-speed shuttle lift to the 350m Tembo Deck.
Walk up the glass-slotted spiral Tembo Galleria at 450 meters.
Savor a sushi or tendon lunch at Tokyo Solamachi dining hall.
Explore the Sumida Aquarium or Planetarium inside the Solamachi complex.

notes: Best visibility of Mt. Fuji occurs on crisp, clear winter mornings.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Tokyo Skytree via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Tokyo Tower
description: Built in 1958 and standing 332.9 meters tall, Tokyo Tower is the beloved red-and-white lattice symbol of post-war Tokyo, offering glass-floor lookouts and romantic night views.
reservation: Top Deck Tour requires reserved time-slot tickets.
parking: Paid parking lot adjacent to Shiba Park at the tower base.

itineraries.name: Classic Tokyo Tower & Shiba Park Sunset Walk
itineraries.description: Visit the iconic lattice tower and enjoy Zojoji Temple sunset views.

steps.activity: Arrive at Akabanebashi Station and walk through Shiba Park.
Take the main elevator up to the 150m Main Deck and stand on the Lookdown Window glass floor.
Watch the sun set over the Tokyo skyline as the tower lights switch on.
Dine at FootTown food hall before taking night photos of Zojoji Temple.

notes: Special illumination colors light up the tower on Friday and Saturday evenings.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Tokyo Tower via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Yokohama Marine Tower
description: Built in 1961 to mark Yokohama Port's 100th anniversary, this 106-meter historic steel lattice lighthouse tower offers sweeping vistas over Yokohama Bay Bridge and Minato Mirai.
parking: Municipal paid parking spaces along Yamashita Park waterfront.

itineraries.name: Yokohama Harbor & Marine Tower Promenade
itineraries.description: Stroll Yamashita Park waterfront and ascend the lighthouse observation deck.

steps.activity: Arrive at Motomachi-Chukagai Station and walk through Yamashita Park.
Ride the glass elevator to the 106m observation floor for 360-degree port views.
Enjoy afternoon tea at the tower concourse lounge.
Walk 5 minutes into Yokohama Chinatown for dinner.

notes: Recognized as a Lover's Sanctuary with special romantic evening light projections.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Yokohama Marine Tower via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Chiba Port Tower
description: Clad in over 5,500 heat-reflecting glass mirrors, this 125-meter waterfront tower stands in Chiba Port Park, offering panoramic sunset views across Tokyo Bay to Mt. Fuji.
parking: Free parking lot at Chiba Port Park (over 200 spaces).

itineraries.name: Chiba Waterfront & Mirror Tower Sunset
itineraries.description: Relax in Chiba Port Park and photograph sunset reflection over Tokyo Bay.

steps.activity: Arrive at Chiba-Minato Station and walk through Chiba Port Park.
Take the glass elevator up to the 4th floor observation deck (113m).
Watch sunset over Tokyo Bay and Mt. Fuji from the oceanview cafe.
Stroll through illuminated park gardens before returning.

notes: Special Christmas illumination features a giant 100m tall light-bulb tree on the glass facade.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Chiba Port Tower via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Oarai Marine Tower
description: Standing 60 meters tall on the Ibaraki coastline, this distinctive triangular glass observation tower overlooks Oarai Port and the vast Pacific Ocean.
parking: Free parking lot at Oarai Marine Tower plaza.

itineraries.name: Oarai Coastal Tower & Seafood Excursion
itineraries.description: Enjoy Pacific ocean views and fresh seafood near Oarai shrine.

steps.activity: Arrive at Oarai Station and walk 15 mins to Marine Tower plaza.
Ascend 55 meters to the top glass observation deck for ocean views.
Walk 3 minutes to Oarai Fish Market for fresh grilled oysters and kaisen-don.
Visit nearby Oarai Isosaki Shrine ocean Torii gate on the rocky coast.

notes: Famous among anime fans as a key setting in Girls und Panzer.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Oarai, Ibaraki via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Art Tower Mito
description: Designed by Pritzker Prize laureate Arata Isozaki to commemorate Mito's 100th municipal anniversary, this 100-meter helix tower is composed of 57 titanium tetrahedrons spiraling skyward.
reservation: No reservation required for tower lookout.
parking: Underground paid parking lot below Art Tower Mito plaza.

itineraries.name: Architectural & Art Discovery in Mito
itineraries.description: Ascend the spiral titanium tower and browse contemporary art galleries.

steps.activity: Arrive at Mito Station and bus 10 mins to Art Tower Mito.
Ride the central glass elevator inside the helix tower to the 86m lookout.
Explore the Contemporary Art Gallery Mito exhibitions.
Enjoy coffee and cake at the plaza cafe overlooking the pipe organ hall.

notes: The tower contains round porthole windows providing unique framed perspectives of Mito City.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Art Tower Mito via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Chubu Electric Power MIRAI TOWER
description: Completed in 1954 as Japan's first TV broadcasting tower, this 180-meter Registered Tangible Cultural Property in Nagoya's Hisaya-odori Park features outdoor observation decks and projection mapping art.
reservation: No reservation required for observation decks.
parking: Underground paid parking structure at Hisaya-odori Park.

itineraries.name: Sakae Park & MIRAI TOWER Evening Exploration
itineraries.description: Stroll Hisaya-odori Park and watch Sakae night lights from the 100m Sky Balcony.

steps.activity: Arrive at Sakae Station and walk through RAYARD Hisaya-odori Park.
Ascend to the 90m Indoor Sky Deck for sunset views.
Step onto the 100m outdoor Sky Balcony to feel open breezes.
Dine at Oasis 21 subterranean dining arcade.

notes: Illuminated every night with energy-efficient LED light displays.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Nagoya TV Tower via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Higashiyama Sky Tower
description: Perched atop an 80m hill inside Higashiyama Zoo & Botanical Gardens, this 134-meter pencil tower reaches a total elevation of 214 meters, offering panoramic views of Central Japan Alps.
parking: Large paid parking lots serving Higashiyama Zoo & Sky Tower.

itineraries.name: Higashiyama Gardens & Sky Tower Sunset
itineraries.description: Explore botanical gardens and take in Japan Top 100 night views.

steps.activity: Arrive at Higashiyama Koen Station and walk through botanical gardens.
Ascend the Sky Tower elevator to the 100m observation deck.
Dine at Sky Restaurant NAGOYA758 overlooking Nagoya city lights.
Photograph the illuminated night panorama certified among Japan Top 100 Night Views.

notes: Combination tickets available for both Higashiyama Zoo and Sky Tower.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Higashiyama Sky Tower via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Befco Bakauke Observatory (Toki Messe)
description: Located on the 31st floor of Toki Messe (140.5m high), this free-admission 360-degree observation deck is the highest viewpoint along the Sea of Japan coast.
parking: Multilevel paid parking garage at Toki Messe convention complex.

itineraries.name: Niigata Port & Toki Messe Sky Walk
itineraries.description: Enjoy free 360-degree views over Shinano River and Sado Island.

steps.activity: Arrive at Niigata Station and bus 10 mins to Toki Messe.
Take the express elevator to the 31st floor Befco Bakauke Observatory Deck.
Spot Sado Island across the Sea of Japan and watch ships enter Shinano River.
Enjoy coffee and local Niigata rice cracker snacks at Sky Lounge Panorama.

notes: Entry is completely free of charge 365 days a year.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Toki Messe via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Nagoya Port Building (Maritime Tower)
description: Designed in the shape of a white ocean sailboat, this 63-meter maritime tower offers 360-degree views over Nagoya Port, Meiko Triton bridges, and cargo container docks.
parking: Paid parking lot at Nagoya Port Garden plaza.

itineraries.name: Nagoya Port & Maritime Heritage Walk
itineraries.description: Ascend the sailboat tower and tour the historic Antarctic icebreaker ship Fuji.

steps.activity: Arrive at Nagoyako Station and walk 3 mins to Nagoya Port Building.
Ride the elevator to the 53m top observation deck for harbor vistas.
Tour the Nagoya Maritime Museum inside the tower complex.
Step aboard the permanently docked Antarctic icebreaker ship Fuji.

notes: Four-facility discount ticket available combining Port Building, Icebreaker Fuji, Aquarium, and Maritime Museum.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Port of Nagoya via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Edo Castle Ruins (Imperial Palace)
description: The historic seat of the Tokugawa Shogunate and current Imperial Palace, featuring massive cyclopean stone ramparts, historic watchtowers, and the Imperial Palace East Gardens.
reservation: Free admission to East Gardens; Imperial Palace Inner Grounds require advance household registration.
parking: Paid parking structures near Tokyo Station and Marunouchi.

itineraries.name: Shogunate Heritage & Gardens Walk
itineraries.description: Explore massive stone ramparts and historic watchtowers of Tokugawa Shogunate headquarters.

steps.activity: Enter through the historic Otemon Gate into Imperial Palace East Gardens.
Climb the Tenshudai (main keep base stone platform) for garden vistas.
Admire the three-story Fujimi-yagura, one of the few surviving Edo-period watchtowers.
Stroll down Wadakura Fountain Park for a French bistro lunch.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #21. Free admission 5 days a week (closed Mondays and Fridays).

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Edo Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Kawagoe Castle Honmaru Goten
description: Built in 1848, this rare structure is the only surviving original samurai lord's Honmaru Goten palace in eastern Japan, featuring tatami council chambers and garden verandas.
parking: Free parking lot opposite Kawagoe City Museum.

itineraries.name: Kawagoe Samurai Palace & Little Edo Walk
itineraries.description: Tour Japan's rare surviving Honmaru Goten palace and historic merchant quarter.

steps.activity: Arrive at Hon-Kawagoe Station and bus 10 mins to Kawagoe Castle.
Walk the polished wooden corridors and Karo council room of Honmaru Goten.
Savor eel unaju at a 150-year-old Kawagoe unagi restaurant.
Explore the Kurazukuri warehouse zone and Toki no Kane bell tower.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #19. Admission is only 100 JPY.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Kawagoe Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Sakura Castle Park & Ruins
description: Constructed in 1611 by Doi Toshikatsu, Sakura Castle features impressive preserved earthen ramparts, deep dry moats, and over 1,100 cherry trees surrounding Rekihaku museum.
reservation: No reservation required for castle park.
parking: Free parking lot at Sakura Castle Park.

itineraries.name: Sakura Castle Earthworks & History Walk
itineraries.description: Hike deep dry moats and visit the National Museum of Japanese History.

steps.activity: Arrive at Keisei Sakura Station and walk 15 mins to Sakura Castle Park.
Explore the massive dry moats and earthen rampart ruins.
Enjoy a traditional bento or soba lunch inside the park.
Tour the National Museum of Japanese History located on the castle grounds.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #20. One of Kanto's finest cherry blossom viewing spots in early April.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Sakura Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Mito Castle Ruins & Kodokan
description: The seat of the Mito Tokugawa family, featuring the restored wooden Otemon Gate, dry moat earthworks, and the 1841 Kodokan—Japan's largest Mito clan samurai school.
parking: Paid parking lot at Kodokan and Mito Station.

itineraries.name: Mito Tokugawa Heritage & Kodokan Walk
itineraries.description: Tour the grand samurai school and walk the restored Mito Castle Otemon gate.

steps.activity: Arrive at Mito Station and walk 8 mins to Kodokan samurai school.
Walk through the newly restored wooden Otemon Castle Gate.
Enjoy Mito natto specialty dishes at a local restaurant.
Stroll to nearby Kairakuen Garden (one of Japan's Three Great Gardens).

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #14. Beautiful plum blossoms bloom in Kodokan grounds every February-March.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Mito Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Tsutsujigasaki Castle (Takeda Shrine)
description: The fortified residence of legendary Sengoku warlord Takeda Shingen, featuring original earthwork ramparts, triple moats, and Takeda Shrine.
parking: Free parking lot at Takeda Shrine entrance.

itineraries.name: Takeda Shingen Stronghold Walk
itineraries.description: Explore warlord Shingen's castle moats and sample Yamanashi Houtou noodles.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kofu Station and bus 10 mins to Takeda Shrine.
Cross the moat bridge and explore original 16th-century stone walls.
Visit the Treasure Museum displaying Shingen's battle armor and sword.
Dine at Koshu Houtou Fudo near Kofu Station for flat iron-pot udon.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #24. Shingen-ko Festival held every April features 1,000 armor-clad samurai parade.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Takeda Shrine via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Hikone Castle (National Treasure)
description: Completed in 1622, Hikone Castle is one of Japan's five original surviving National Treasure keeps, overlooking Lake Biwa with pristine feudal turrets and Genkyuen garden.
parking: Paid parking lot at Hikone Castle Otemae.

itineraries.name: National Treasure Keep & Genkyuen Walk
itineraries.description: Climb the authentic 1622 wooden keep and stroll Genkyuen garden overlooking Lake Biwa.

steps.activity: Arrive at Hikone Station and walk 10 mins to Hikone Castle.
Climb the steep internal wooden ladders of the National Treasure Keep.
Watch the daily Hikonyan mascot performance at the castle museum.
Savor Omi Beef steak or sukiyaki lunch in Yumekyobashi Castle Road.
Stroll around Genkyuen feudal garden reflecting the castle tower on its pond.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #50. Steep wooden stairs inside keep require sturdy footwear.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Hikone Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Gifu Castle (Mt. Kinka)
description: Perched 329 meters atop Mt. Kinka, Gifu Castle was Oda Nobunaga's formidable mountain stronghold, offering panoramic views over the Nagara River and Nobi Plain.
parking: Paid parking lot at Gifu Park ropeway station base.

itineraries.name: Mt. Kinka Summit & Nobunaga Fortress Excursion
itineraries.description: Ride the ropeway to Oda Nobunaga's mountain summit castle.

steps.activity: Arrive at JR Gifu Station and bus 15 mins to Gifu Park.
Ride the Mt. Kinka Ropeway to the mountain peak.
Climb to Gifu Castle observation deck for 360-degree views of Nobi Plain.
Enjoy Gifu sweetfish (Ayu) or Hida beef lunch in Gifu Park.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #39. Special night ropeway operates during summer weekends for night views.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Gifu Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Ueda Castle Park
description: Built in 1583 by Sanada Masayuki, this impregnable fortress famously repelled the massive Tokugawa army twice, featuring surviving corner turrets and Sanada Shrine.
parking: Paid parking lot at Ueda Castle Park entrance.

itineraries.name: Sanada Warlord Heritage & Castle Walk
itineraries.description: Explore the legendary Sanada clan fortress and sample Shinshu Shinano Soba.

steps.activity: Arrive at Ueda Station (Hokuriku Shinkansen) and walk 12 mins to Ueda Castle.
Tour the South and North Yagura turrets and the massive Sanada Stone wall.
Pay respects at Sanada Shrine inside the inner castle grounds.
Enjoy famous Ueda Shinshu buckwheat soba noodles at a historic shop.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #27. Spectacular cherry blossom festival every mid-April.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Ueda Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Takato Castle Park
description: Renowned as one of Japan's top three cherry blossom viewing spots, Takato Castle ruins feature 1,500 rare reddish Kohigan cherry trees frame over ancient stone moats.
parking: Large temporary parking lots open during cherry blossom festival.

itineraries.name: Takato Sakura & Castle Moat Walk
itineraries.description: Experience Japan's legendary Kohigan cherry blossom tunnel above castle moats.

steps.activity: Arrive at Ina-shi Station and bus 25 mins to Takato Castle Park.
Walk across the curved Ohoribashi bridge shrouded in pink blossoms.
Enjoy Takato Soba with spicy radish soup at a park teahouse.
Explore the Taikoyagura drum tower and castle moat ramparts.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #28. Cherry blossoms bloom mid-to-late April.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Takatō Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Kakegawa Castle & Ninomaru Goten
description: Rebuilt in 1994 using traditional Japanese carpentry techniques, Kakegawa Castle is Japan's first authentically reconstructed wooden keep, featuring the original 1855 Ninomaru Goten palace.
parking: Paid parking lot near Kakegawa Castle park.

itineraries.name: Kakegawa Wooden Keep & Green Tea Walk
itineraries.description: Climb the authentic wooden keep and savor premium Shizuoka green tea.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kakegawa Station (Tokaido Shinkansen) and walk 8 mins to the castle.
Climb the traditional cypress wood steps of the reconstructed 1994 keep.
Explore the original Ninomaru Goten samurai lord palace.
Enjoy matcha and wagashi sweets at Ninomaru Tea House overlooking castle gardens.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #42. Ninomaru Goten is an Important Cultural Property.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Kakegawa Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Kanazawa Castle Park
description: The magnificent seat of the powerful Maeda clan (Kaga Domain), featuring meticulously reconstructed wooden turrets, white lead tile roofs, and Gyokusen'inmaru feudal garden adjacent to Kenrokuen.
parking: Paid parking lot at Kenrokuen / Kanazawa Castle Park entrance.

itineraries.name: Kaga Domain Power & Kenrokuen Garden Walk
itineraries.description: Explore Maeda clan turrets and walk across to Kenrokuen Garden.

steps.activity: Arrive at Kanazawa Station (Hokuriku Shinkansen) and bus 10 mins to Ishikawamon.
Tour inside the reconstructed Hishi Yagura and Gojukkoku Nagaya wooden storehouse.
Savor Kaga Kaiseki or fresh Omicho Market kaisen-don lunch.
Walk across the bridge directly into Kenrokuen Garden (Japan #1 Garden).

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #35. Beautiful night illumination of Gyokusen'inmaru garden on weekends.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Kanazawa Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Maruoka Castle (Original Keep 1576)
description: Built in 1576, Maruoka Castle is one of Japan's 12 original surviving castle keeps and features the oldest architectural keep style in Japan, complete with rare stone roof tiles.
parking: Free parking lot at Maruoka Castle Kasumi Park.

itineraries.name: Maruoka Original Keep & Kasumi Park Walk
itineraries.description: Climb Japan's oldest surviving keep style with stone roof shingles.

steps.activity: Arrive at Fukui Station (Hokuriku Shinkansen) and bus 40 mins to Maruoka Castle.
Climb the steep 67-degree wooden stairs inside the 1576 original keep.
Enjoy Fukui Echizen soba noodles with grated daikon radish.
Stroll Kasumi Park surrounding the castle hill.

notes: Top 100 Castle of Japan #36. Known as Kasumi-ga-jyo (Mist Castle) due to legend of fog concealing the tower from enemies.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Maruoka Castle via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building Observatories
description: Perched 202 meters above Shinjuku on the 45th floors of Kenzo Tange iconic twin-tower skyscraper, offering free 360-degree views of Mt. Fuji and Tokyo lights.
parking: Underground paid parking garage at Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building.

itineraries.name: Free Shinjuku Sky Observatory & Sunset
itineraries.description: Enjoy free 360-degree vistas over Tokyo skyline and Mt. Fuji from 202 meters high.

steps.activity: Arrive at Tochomae Station and ride express elevator to 45F South Deck.
Watch sunset over Mt. Fuji from the high-altitude glass perimeter.
Listen to street piano performances on the famous memory piano.
Dine at the 45th floor sky dining lounge overlooking evening city lights.

notes: Free entry 365 days a year. South Deck open until 10:00 PM.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Tokyo Metropolitan Government Building via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Shibuya Sky (Rooftop Observatory)
description: Perched 229 meters directly above Shibuya Crossing atop Shibuya Scramble Square, featuring an open-air rooftop glass-edge deck with 360-degree views of Tokyo Tower and Mt. Fuji.
reservation: Advance online ticket booking required 2 to 4 weeks prior.
parking: Underground paid parking at Shibuya Scramble Square.

itineraries.name: Shibuya Open-Air Sky Deck & Scramble View
itineraries.description: Experience Japan's famous open-air rooftop observatory over Shibuya Scramble.

steps.activity: Arrive at Shibuya Station and take express elevator to 14F ticket gate.
Step onto the 47F open-air rooftop lawn and pose at the glass Sky Edge corner.
Watch sunset over Mt. Fuji and Shibuya Crossing lights coming alive below.
Enjoy cocktails at Paradise Lounge 46F.

notes: Rooftop deck may close temporarily during heavy rain or strong wind.

name: Roppongi Hills Observation Deck (Tokyo City View)
description: Located on the 52nd floor and rooftop of Mori Tower (270m height), offering the iconic front-row view of Tokyo Tower framed against the Tokyo Bay skyline.
parking: Paid underground parking structure at Roppongi Hills Mori Tower.

itineraries.name: Tokyo Tower Skyline Sunset & Mori Art Museum
itineraries.description: Combine contemporary art with Tokyo's premier Tokyo Tower night view.

steps.activity: Arrive at Roppongi Station and ascend Mori Tower to 53F Mori Art Museum.
Walk down to 52F Tokyo City View as twilight illuminates Tokyo Tower.
Take open-air Sky Deck helipad elevator to feel 270m breezes.
Dine at Roppongi Hills Hillside gourmet arcade.

notes: The ticket includes entry to both Tokyo City View and Mori Art Museum exhibitions.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Roppongi Hills Mori Tower via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Sunshine 60 Observatory (Tenbou Park)
description: Located on the 60th floor of Sunshine 60 (251m height) in Ikebukuro, featuring an indoor sky park with real turf lawns, picnic mats, and panoramic vistas.
parking: Paid parking garage inside Sunshine City complex.

itineraries.name: Ikebukuro Indoor Sky Picnic & Aquarium
itineraries.description: Relax on high-altitude turf lawns and visit Sunshine City rooftop aquarium.

steps.activity: Arrive at Ikebukuro Station and walk 8 mins through Sunshine 60 Street.
Ride the high-speed elevator to the 60th floor Tenbou Park observatory.
Relax on the indoor artificial grass hill with seasonal drinks from Tenbou Cafe.
Visit Sunshine Aquarium on the rooftop of the main building.

notes: Completely indoor and rainproof; perfect for families with young children.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Sunshine 60 via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Yokohama Landmark Tower (Sky Garden)
description: Ascend to the 69th floor (273m) in just 40 seconds aboard Japan's fastest elevator for 360-degree panoramas of Yokohama Bay Bridge, Minato Mirai, and Mt. Fuji.
parking: Paid underground parking structure at Yokohama Landmark Tower.

itineraries.name: Yokohama Harbor Sky High Excursion
itineraries.description: Ride Japan's fastest elevator and take in Minato Mirai harbor night lights.

steps.activity: Arrive at Sakuragicho Station and walk 5 mins via moving walkway.
Board the 750m/min elevator to 69F Sky Garden observatory deck.
Savor twilight sunset over Mt. Fuji and Yokohama Cosmo Clock Ferris wheel.
Dine at Landmark Plaza gourmet dining deck.

notes: Elevator reaches 69F in 40 seconds (750m/min top speed).

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Yokohama Landmark Tower via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Abeno Harukas 300 (Osaka Skyline)
description: Rising 300 meters above Osaka, Abeno Harukas is Japan's tallest skyscraper, featuring a 3-story glass observatory and open-air wooden atrium garden.
parking: Paid parking garage at Abeno Harukas building.

itineraries.name: Osaka 300m Sky High Vista & Dining
itineraries.description: Stand 300 meters above Osaka plain and dine at the open-air wooden atrium.

steps.activity: Arrive at Osaka-Abenobashi Station and take shuttle elevator to 16F.
Ride express elevator to 60F 360-degree glass corridor.
Relax at 58F open-air wooden deck garden with gelato or craft beer.
Dine at Abeno Harukas Kintetsu gourmet dining hall 12F-14F.

notes: Features 360-degree glass floor-to-ceiling perimeter views.

imageMetadata.attribution: Lead photograph of Abeno Harukas via Wikipedia/Wikimedia Commons

name: Kairakuen Garden
description: Kairakuen in Mito is one of Japan's Three Great Gardens, famous for its over 3,000 plum trees of 100 different varieties that bloom brilliantly in early spring.
notes: Founded in 1842 by Tokugawa Nariaki, designed as a public park for citizens to relax.

name: Korakuen Garden
description: Korakuen in Okayama is a celebrated landscape garden built by feudal lord Ikeda Tsunamasa in 1700, featuring spacious lawns, ponds, tea trees, and borrowed scenery of Okayama Castle.
notes: Located right across the Asahi River from Okayama Castle.

name: Himeji Castle (White Heron Castle)
description: Himeji Castle is Japan's premier surviving feudal castle complex and a UNESCO World Heritage site, famous for its brilliant white plaster walls resembling a taking-off heron.
notes: Completed in 1609, it survived World War II bombings and major earthquakes completely intact.

name: Hirosaki Castle
description: Hirosaki Castle in Aomori is the northernmost original keep in Japan, surrounded by over 2,600 cherry trees making it one of Japan's most celebrated spring destinations.
notes: Built in 1611 by the Tsugaru clan.

name: Matsue Castle
description: Matsue Castle, completed in 1611, is one of Japan's five castles designated as National Treasures, featuring striking black wooden siding nicknamed the Black Plover Castle.
notes: Surrounded by historic moat boat tours (Horikawa Pleasure Boat).

name: Kochi Castle
description: Kochi Castle is unique among Japan's 12 original keep castles for retaining both its main keep and the original feudal palace (Honmaru Goten) intact.
notes: Built by Yamauchi Kazutoyo following the Battle of Sekigahara.

name: Bitchu Matsuyama Castle
description: Bitchu Matsuyama Castle is Japan's highest original mountain castle (430 meters above sea level), celebrated for floating above a sea of clouds on autumn mornings.
notes: Built in 1240, the current keep dates to 1683.

name: Marugame Castle
description: Marugame Castle features Japan's tallest combined stone wall foundations (60 meters high in total), supporting a compact original three-story wooden keep.
notes: Famous for its sweeping curve stone wall technique known as Ogura-tsumi.

name: Iyo Matsuyama Castle
description: Iyo Matsuyama Castle is one of Japan's most complex surviving hilltop castles, accessible via scenic chairlift or ropeway overlooking the Seto Inland Sea.
notes: Features 21 historically designated important cultural properties.

name: Uwajima Castle
description: Uwajima Castle, constructed by master castle architect Todo Takatora, stands atop a coastal hill in southern Ehime with its pristine three-story original keep.
notes: Preserved by the Date clan who ruled Uwajima domain throughout the Edo period.

name: Mount Hakodate Night View
description: Mount Hakodate offers one of Japan's Three Great Night Views, overlooking a stunning hourglass-shaped peninsula illuminated between Tsugaru Strait and Hakodate Bay.
notes: Accessible via the Mount Hakodate Ropeway in just 3 minutes.

name: Mount Maya Kikuseidai (Kobe)
description: Known as the Ten Million Dollar Night View, Kikuseidai observatory on Mount Maya captures a breathtaking sparkling panorama spanning Kobe, Osaka Bay, and Kansai.
notes: Kikuseidai literally means a platform where you can scoop up stars with your hands.

name: Gero Onsen
description: Gero Onsen is one of Japan's Three Great Hot Springs (Nihon Sanmeisen), famed since the 9th century for its silky alkaline waters known as 'beauty baths'.
notes: Situated along the Hida River between Nagoya and Takayama.

name: Arima Onsen
description: Arima Onsen is Japan's oldest recorded hot spring resort, nestled behind Mount Rokko and famous for its iron-rich golden 'Kinsen' and clear radium 'Ginsen' waters.
notes: Favored by Toyotomi Hideyoshi who built private baths here.

name: Kinosaki Onsen
description: Kinosaki Onsen is a quintessential hot spring town centered around a willow-lined canal, where visitors stroll between seven distinct public bathhouses in traditional yukata robes.
notes: Founded over 1,300 years ago when a stork was observed healing its wounded leg in the thermal springs.

name: Historic Kyoto (Kiyomizu-dera & Kinkaku-ji)
description: Kyoto, Japan's imperial capital for over a millennium, encompasses 17 UNESCO World Heritage monuments including the wooden balcony of Kiyomizu-dera and the shimmering Golden Pavilion.
notes: Home to over 2,000 temples and shrines.

name: Historic Nara (Todai-ji Great Buddha)
description: Nara Park houses the world's largest wooden building (Todai-ji Daibutsuden), sheltering the 15-meter bronze Great Buddha alongside over 1,200 friendly freely roaming sika deer.
notes: Nara was Japan's first permanent capital (710–784 AD).

name: Itsukushima Shrine (Miyajima Island)
description: Itsukushima Shrine on Miyajima Island is world-renowned for its iconic vermilion O-Torii gate that appears to float magically upon the Seto Inland Sea during high tide.
notes: Designated as one of the Three Views of Japan (Nihon Sankei).

name: Matsushima Bay
description: Matsushima Bay is one of Japan's Three Great Views (Nihon Sankei), celebrated for over 260 pine-clad islands scattered across tranquil coastal waters.
notes: Famed poet Matsuo Basho was left speechless by its beauty in Oku no Hosomichi.

name: Amanohashidate (Bridge to Heaven)
description: Amanohashidate is a natural 3.3-kilometer pine-covered sandbar spanning Miyazu Bay, famed for the Matanozoki upside-down viewing technique making it look like a bridge floating into heaven.
notes: Planted with nearly 7,000 pine trees.

name: Kegon Falls (Nikko)
description: Kegon Falls is Japan's most famous 97-meter waterfall, carrying the outlet waters of Lake Chuzenji down a dramatic cliff in Nikko National Park.
notes: Accessible via an 100-meter underground elevator to the lower observation deck.

name: Nachi Falls & Pagoda
description: Nachi Falls is Japan's tallest uninterrupted single-drop waterfall (133 meters), framing the three-story red Seiganto-ji pagoda against the sacred Nachi primeval forest.
notes: Part of the UNESCO Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range.

name: Ise Grand Shrine (Ise Jingu)
description: Ise Grand Shrine is the spiritual heart of Shinto, dedicated to the Sun Goddess Amaterasu and rebuilt every 20 years in accordance with ancient Shikinen Sengu ritual.
notes: Home to the Sacred Mirror (Yata no Kagami), one of Japan's Three Imperial Regalia.

name: Izumo Taisha Grand Shrine
description: Izumo Taisha is one of Japan's oldest Shinto shrines, famed for its massive 5.2-ton straw Shimenawa rope hanging at the Kaguraden hall and its deity of marriage and destiny.
notes: In October (Kamiarizuki), all 8 million Shinto gods gather at Izumo Taisha.

name: Atsuta Shrine (Nagoya)
description: Atsuta Shrine in central Nagoya is a sacred ancient Shinto sanctuary surrounded by a tranquil cedar forest, enshrining the legendary Kusanagi-no-Tsurugi sacred sword.
notes: Founded nearly 2,000 years ago during the reign of Emperor Keiko.

name: Mount Yoshino (Yoshinoyama)
description: Mount Yoshino is Japan's most famous cherry blossom mountain, carpeted by over 30,000 Yamazakura trees blooming sequentially across four mountain elevations.
notes: Part of the Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range UNESCO site.

name: Mount Inasa (Nagasaki Night View)
description: Mount Inasa in Nagasaki is designated as one of the World's New Three Great Night Views, offering a 360° illuminated bowl view of Nagasaki harbor and surrounding hills.
notes: 333 meters high, connected by Nagasaki Ropeway.

name: Osaka Castle (Osaka-jo)
description: Osaka Castle is an iconic 16th-century fortress constructed by Toyotomi Hideyoshi, featuring golden roof ornaments, massive stone ramparts, and a lush park surrounded by moats.
notes: Reconstructed in 1931 with an elevator-serviced interior museum.

name: Nijo Castle (Kyoto)
description: Nijo Castle in Kyoto was the residence of Tokugawa Ieyasu, famous for its Ninomaru Palace featuring squeaking 'nightingale floors' designed to alert guards to intruders.
notes: Site where Shogun Tokugawa Yoshinobu returned power to Emperor Meiji in 1867.

name: Kumamoto Castle
description: Kumamoto Castle is one of Japan's most formidable castles, engineered by master builder Kato Kiyomasa with steep curved Musha-gaeshi stone walls designed to prevent ninja scaling.
notes: Main keep fully restored following the 2016 Kumamoto earthquakes.
